$comment: SMGP canon-divergence pack (prefix sc.): the career's venue winners measured against the almanac's remembered rulers (what-really-happened.json). SMGP UNIVERSE provenance, the SEGA canon is fiction and these cards keep the two ledgers visibly separate, never merged. Follows 010-core.json format; read-side only.

smgp: THE BIG BOARD KEEPS BOTH LINES.
CANON IN ONE COLUMN, CAREER IN THE OTHER.
THE ALMANAC DOES NOT FLINCH. NEITHER DOES THE TIMING SHEET.
default: Legend and ledger are different books, and the archive shelves both.
What the world remembers and what the season recorded now differ by one name.
The almanac keeps its story; the career keeps its result.

templates.headline: New name over an old legend[[?venue: at {venue}]]
templates.deck: SMGP canon card, season {season}: the remembered ruler is beaten
templates.summary: SMGP legend remembers {rival}[[?venue: ruling {venue}]]; this career filed {winner}.

sections.lead: The almanac's page[[?venue: for {venue}]] names its remembered ruler: {rival}. This career's timing sheet types a different name: {winner}[[?winnerTeam: of {winnerTeam}]]. The archive now keeps both columns side by side.
sections.context: The legend loses nothing; that is how canon works in this universe. But a career is its own book, and its pages get written at racing speed by whoever arrives first.

templates.headline: {winner} rewrites the local legend[[?venue: at {venue}]]
templates.summary: Canon gave this circuit to {rival}; the race answered to {winner}.

sections.lead: Every circuit in this universe has a remembered ruler[[?venue: , and {venue}'s is {rival}]]. Remembered, not eternal. The demonstration came from {winner}[[?winnerTeam: and {winnerTeam}]], in front of everyone.
sections.impact: The old page stays printed; the new page starts an argument. The paddock will happily conduct it all season.

templates.headline: The legend holds[[?venue: at {venue}]]
templates.deck: SMGP canon card, season {season}: the remembered ruler rules again
templates.summary: Canon remembers {rival}[[?venue: ruling {venue}]], and this career's race agreed.

sections.lead: Some pages do not need revising. The almanac remembers {rival}[[?venue: ruling {venue}]], and this career's running of the race reached the same verdict, in the present tense.
